events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: snf-wants-build-plan

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it ONLY if it genuinely fits the request. Treat it as a skill you may invoke if appropriate.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a HALF-BAKED, early-stage, UNVALIDATED software/product idea and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it before designing or building.
WHEN NOT TO USE: the concept is ALREADY VALIDATED and the user wants design/architecture, OR the user wants an IMPLEMENTATION / BUILD PLAN with milestones and tasks (that is the job of a planning skill, not this one).
If you do apply it: run an adaptive Socratic interview — ONE question per message, rate 8 dimensions and show a scorecard, mandatory adversarial stress-test, then write an idea brief and hand off. This skill explicitly does NOT write the execution plan / scope / timeline itself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
The concept is locked and approved. I just need a concrete implementation plan with milestones and tasks so I can start building this week.

Respond as you would to the user. Decide for yourself whether the bake-to-completion skill fits this request or not, and act accordingly. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to true ONLY if you actually applied the bake-to-completion interview skill.

The user wants a concrete implementation plan with milestones and tasks. The skill's "WHEN NOT TO USE" clause is explicit: do not apply it when the user wants an implementation/build plan. The concept is already locked and approved, and the user is not asking for ideation, validation, or pressure-testing. This is a planning request, not an idea-baking request. I will respond as a competent generalist without applying the skill.

```json
{
  "caseId": "snf-wants-build-plan",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "Understood — if you share the concept details (what you are building, the tech stack you plan to use, and any constraints like team size or deadline), I can put together a concrete implementation plan with milestones and tasks you can start executing this week.\n\nTo make the plan immediately actionable, it helps to know:\n\n1. What the product or feature does at a high level (one or two sentences).\n2. The target stack or environment (e.g., Next.js + Postgres, Python FastAPI, mobile, etc.).\n3. Any hard constraints — solo or team, specific deadline, existing codebase to integrate with.\n4. The definition of done for the first working version (MVP scope).\n\nDrop those details and I will structure a phased plan with clear milestones and concrete tasks you can hand to a board or start tackling directly.",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": false,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Read the skill's WHEN NOT TO USE clause",
      "Confirmed the user has a locked, approved concept and wants an execution plan, not ideation or validation",
      "Concluded the bake-to-completion skill does not apply",
      "Responded as a capable generalist by asking for the details needed to produce the requested plan"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user's concept is genuinely finalized and does not need pressure-testing",
      "No concept details were provided yet, so the plan cannot be written without them"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "Without concept details I cannot produce the actual plan; my response asks for those details rather than producing a placeholder plan"
    ]
  }
}
```
